meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for AI Builders: No More Stuck Projects
content.seo_description: Struggling with no project ideas? Use this expert-led project management checklist for freelancers to ship faster with Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea. Use structured project management to turn vague concepts into shippable MVPs. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you transform your workflow from 'stuck' to 'shipped,' ensuring every client engagement results in a high-value, production-ready AI application.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a freelancer's graveyard. You’re staring at the cursor, the deadline is looming, and the 'no project ideas' paralysis is setting in. It’s not a lack of creativity; it’s a failure of systemization. When you lack a rigorous framework, even the best ideas die in the ideation phase.

But what if you didn't have to 'think' of an idea from scratch? By treating project management as a modular engineering task, you can pipeline your development process. We’re going to use Antigravity to architect your vision and Claude Code to execute the boilerplate, turning your workflow into a high-velocity ship factory.

steps.title: Define the Problem-Solution Fit
steps.description: Identify a recurring pain point in a specific vertical. Use Antigravity to map the core feature set that solves this friction point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product manager. Generate 3 high-impact AI SaaS project ideas for small business owners that solve inventory or scheduling problems. For the best one, output a feature list and a technical stack recommendation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project roadmap for a [Project Name] based on the Antigravity feature list.'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: Structure your data before writing logic. A solid schema prevents technical debt later in the project lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS application managing [Project Type]. Include tables for users, subscriptions, and core entities. Use PostgreSQL syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a Prisma schema file based on the provided database design.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Application Boilerplate
steps.description: Use Claude Code to set up your environment, ensuring all dependencies and configuration files are production-ready from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: List the essential boilerplate components needed for a Next.js 14, Tailwind, and Supabase project. Include folder structure and base configuration files.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project with the specified structure and install essential dependencies.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic via Iterative Prompting
steps.description: Break complex features into small, testable chunks. Build the MVP one function at a time to maintain high code quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the core authentication and dashboard logic into 5 discrete, implementable tasks. Provide the logic flow for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the authentication flow using NextAuth and Supabase based on task 1.'

steps.title: Automate Deployment and Review
steps.description: Set up a CI/CD pipeline early. Use Claude Code to review your code for security vulnerabilities before the first deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file to automate deployments to Vercel for this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and suggest best practices for production.'

pro_tips: Treat your prompts as code: Version control your Antigravity prompts in a /docs/prompts folder to make your process repeatable.
Always start with the database schema; if you can't model the data, you don't understand the project well enough to build it.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command on every pull request to maintain a senior-engineer standard without the senior-engineer hours.
When stuck, use Antigravity to 'reverse-engineer' a competitor's feature set into a set of actionable user stories.
Prioritize 'boring' tech stacks; your clients care about reliability and speed, not the trendiest framework of the week.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, push your code to a private GitHub repository, deploy via Vercel, and begin the feedback loop with your first beta user.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build anything. Stop overthinking and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
